$schema_note: RDR-011 ILLUSTRATIVE composite ledger — a worked example of a workflow-level Artifact[] produced by an orchestrator (the /accept-of-an-RDR spine). NOT an enforcement surface: the four-kind union is pinned by agent-shapes.json. This fixture is a prose-companion echo for docs/contracts/harvest-producer-contract.md, validated against the four-kind union AND the producer obligations by harvest-ledger-example.test.ts. It deliberately exercises the hard cases: (a) LOG-NOT-SET (P2/C-2) — the T2 key 'qwen-coprocessor-stack_rdr/RDR-011' appears twice (status written, then re-written after the plan lands) and the bead 'qwen-coprocessor-stack-8xe' appears twice (created, then updated), so a producer that deduplicated at emission would drop entries; (b) the link composite-edge-key encoding (P4) in BOTH the 3-field form ('<from>|<type>|<to>') and the 5-field span form ('<from>|<type>|<to>|<from_span>|<to_span>' with an absent from_span as an empty string → doubled pipe); (c) per-tier key conventions (T1=entry_id, T2/T3='project|collection / title|doc_id'); (d) the entity.op / tier-no-op asymmetry (P5).
scenario: /accept of RDR-011: spine writes T2 status (tier), edits RDR frontmatter+README (patch via git), flips the RDR status entity to updated, mints the planning epic + a task bead, creates two catalog links (one plain, one span-scoped), dispatches a planner leaf that returns plan JSON (value), writes the plan to T2 (tier), then RE-writes the RDR status tier and UPDATEs the epic bead with its child refs — the two repeats demonstrate the append-ordered log.
